https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/thibaud1010/:
⚽ An exercise idea to sharpen your defensive transition reflex and reaction speed.
In a 15×15 m area with a mini-goal on each side, an attacker takes on a defender to dribble and score. The moment they score or lose the ball, a new attacker comes in and the old attacker switches to defending, reacting instantly to stop the goal.
You train your transition reflex, reaction speed and explosiveness the second the role flips.
🎯 1 point per goal, 2 if the defender wins the ball back. Volume: 4-5 reps per role.

⚽ An exercise idea to build quick transitions, reactivity and collective solidarity.
Two mini-goals on the sides plus a big goal with keeper. It starts 2 vs 1: the reds attack the mini-goals, the blue the big goal. When the action ends, two blues join for 3 vs 2, then two reds for 4 vs 3. The team in possession has 10 seconds to score.
You train immediate reactivity to every ball loss and recovery.
🎯 Each phase lasts 1-2 minutes before a new sequence.

⚽ An intermittent session to lift your MAS while adding footwork strength and proprioception.
Split into two groups. Set out 30-40 m running lanes plus plyometric and balance stations: single-leg holds, jumps with a stop, lateral and multidirectional jumps. One group works the stations while the other runs, in a 20-20 format (20 sec effort / 20 sec recovery).
You train maximal aerobic speed, explosive strength and ankle stability together.
🎯 4-minute cycle, then swap. Do 2 sets.

⚽ An exercise idea to develop maximum speed, reactivity and the ability to accelerate.
Players stand on cones about 10 m apart, backs turned to the running direction. On the signal, player 1 sprints flat out in a straight line. As soon as they overtake another player, that player instantly takes off to chase them down, and so on down the line.
You train pure top-end speed and an explosive reaction off a standing start.
🎯 Switch cones each rep. Minimum 1 minute between reps.

⚽ An exercise idea to build footwork solidity, core strength and the power to unbalance an opponent.
Pairs face off in a small marked area, holding hands. On the signal, each player pushes, pulls and tries to unbalance the other; the aim is to shove your opponent out of the zone, sumo-style.
You train core stability, grip on the ground and raw duelling strength.
🎯 Short, intense bouts of 15-30 sec for 7-8 minutes. Rotate roles and opponents.

⚽ An exercise idea to develop maximum speed and the counter-attack.
Three defenders (one on the goal line, two on the halfway line) and two attackers starting from halfway. The two attackers must sprint hard to keep it 2 vs 1 so the central defender can't recover; too slow and it becomes 2 vs 3. No tackling for the central defenders, who can only defend once they've got ahead of the attackers.
You train explosive acceleration in a race against the recovery.
🎯 Maximum 10 seconds to score. 3-4 reps in each role.

⚽ An exercise idea to combine reaction speed, explosiveness and maximum speed.
Two attackers start from the red cones against two defenders, one on the goal line and one on a blue cone near the centre. Start fast and it's 2 vs 1; too slow and the central defender recovers to make it 2 vs 2. No tackling for the central defender, who can only defend once he's overtaken the attackers.
You train the explosive first stride and top speed under pressure.
🎯 Maximum 10 seconds to score. 3-4 reps per role. Minimum 1 minute recovery between reps.

⚽ An exercise idea to mix reaction speed, a short sprint and decision-making in a fun, competitive format.
Two teams face each other, each player with a number, around a 3×3 tic-tac-toe grid on the ground. On the signal the coach calls a number: those players sprint in and each claims a free square. A new number is called, and so on; the goal is to line up three players, horizontally, vertically or diagonally.
You train reaction speed, short explosive sprints and quick reading.
🎯 5-6 reps. Minimum 30 seconds between reps.

⚽ An exercise idea to sharpen reaction speed and explosive movement in a fun, competitive format.
Each player stands inside a hoop, with different-coloured poles around them; before each start the coach removes a hoop, so one player is always left without a spot. On the signal a colour is called: players leave their hoop, go round the matching pole and race back to any free hoop. The one left out is eliminated, and it repeats with one hoop fewer.
You train visual reaction and explosive changes of direction.
🎯 Minimum 45 seconds between reps.

⚽ An exercise idea to develop quickness and agility in a competitive format.
Four stations with varied kit, each roughly the same length: red, frequency and jumps over hurdles and ladder; green, sprint to a pole, walk backward and sprint again; yellow, slalom between poles; blue, sprint and change direction round the cones. The winner of each station scores a point, and the player with most points after the four wins.
You train foot speed, agility and change of direction.
🎯 1 point per station won. Minimum 45 seconds between reps.

⚽ An exercise idea to build aerobic capacity with the ball across five technical circuits linked without a pause.
Five ball circuits run one after another: left- then right-foot slaloms with a backward walk in the last square; slaloms through poles into an acceleration; a both-footed slalom through hoops, passing under the hurdles while jumping them, then ladder footwork keeping control; a fast slalom into an acceleration between the yellow cones.
You keep your aerobic engine ticking over while looking after the ball throughout.

⚽ An exercise idea to build aerobic capacity while linking coordination, proprioception and finishing.
Three groups, one per station. Station 1: send the ball after the agility ladder for coordination, then drive through the cones (yellow left foot, blue right). Station 2: ladder footwork, an acceleration, single-leg proprioception on a cushion or bosu, then footwork in the hoops. Station 3: accelerate with the ball into the box and shoot.
You train your aerobic engine, balance and finishing in one linked loop.

⚽ An exercise idea to build aerobic capacity in pairs, combining passing quality, coordination and finishing.
In twos, one station each. Station 1: pass to each other through the poles toward station 2 (red poles left foot, yellow right). Station 2: one going forward, one backward, exchanging the ball with varied touches. Station 3: one accelerates with the ball to the corner and feeds a teammate near the box, who controls and shoots.
You sustain aerobic work while keeping pass and finish quality high.
🎯 Laps run non-stop for 15 minutes.

⚽ An exercise idea to develop aerobic endurance while adding technical precision across seven accuracy stations.
Move through the seven: knock down a cone, get as close as possible to a pole (like pointing in pétanque), hit the crossbar, drop the ball inside a hoop, knock down another cone, close in on a pole again, and pass the ball under a hurdle set in the middle of the goal.
You keep a steady aerobic load while demanding fine striking precision.
🎯 1 point per exercise completed. No stopping, moderate pace, 15-20 minutes.

⚽ An exercise idea to hold high aerobic capacity through collective pass-and-follow, punctuated with shots on goal.
Pass and follow: players set off together from "1" and "2", pass, then take their partner's place with no stopping. Once they finish the red circuit they switch to the blue one, and vice versa. Cue the chain control and quick pass, the check-and-go before receiving, and a look at your partner before you play.
You train continuous aerobic work with sharp passing on the move.

⚽ An exercise idea to develop aerobic power in a 10-20 intermittent format over varied circuits.
On half a pitch, set several different circuits with turns, brakes and changes of direction. Each player runs their circuit at high intensity: 10 seconds of effort at around 100% MAS, then 20 seconds of active recovery, jogging or fast walking. After each recovery, players switch circuit.
You train maximal aerobic power alongside agility and braking.
🎯 Total 6 to 10 minutes depending on level.

⚽ An exercise idea to develop aerobic power and team-block cohesion through demanding small-sided games.
On half a pitch, 6 vs 6 or 7 vs 7, with the space split into several zones (here four). The key rule: a team must always keep all its players within two adjacent zones; if anyone strays into another zone, the team loses the ball. That forces the block to shift and stay compact together.
You train repeated high-intensity efforts while holding collective shape.
🎯 2 × 6 minutes.

⚽ An exercise idea to combine aerobic power and strength with explosive moves on colour cues.
Set out several squares of different sizes with four coloured cones at the corners. Players work in pairs: one active, the other recovering and calling the cues. When the resting player calls a colour, their partner reacts and sprints to touch the matching cone as fast as possible.
You train aerobic power and explosive reactivity in the same effort.
🎯 30-30 format (30 sec effort / 30 sec recovery) for 8 minutes.

⚽ An exercise idea to develop aerobic power position by position, with circuits matched to each line's movement.
Each player runs a position-specific circuit alternating 10 seconds at around 100% MAS and 20 seconds of active recovery. Full-back: a straight run with a hurdle jump, touch a pole and return. Midfielder: changes of direction forward and back over 10-15 m. Forward: a slalom simulating an off-the-ball run and a run in behind.
You train match-specific aerobic power in your own movement patterns.
🎯 7 to 10 minutes.

⚽ An exercise idea to build the ability to repeat efforts while constantly reading information.
Place several coloured cones in a zone, players at the start. In a 20-20 format, during each 20-second effort the coach calls two or three colours; players react quickly and touch the cones in the exact order called, with sharp movements and changes of direction, then recover for 20 seconds.
You train repeated efforts combined with quick decision-making under fatigue.
🎯 1 to 2 sets of 5 to 8 minutes.

⚽ An exercise idea to load muscular strength and aerobic endurance at once, in intermittent format.
Two linked parts. Part 1 (3 minutes): chain strength stations in 10 seconds work / 20 seconds recovery, including horizontal and vertical jumps. Part 2 (4 minutes): MAS running in a 15-15 format, 15 seconds high intensity and 15 seconds recovery.
You train explosive strength and your aerobic engine in the same block.
🎯 3 minutes of strength, then 4 minutes of running.

⚽ An exercise idea to alternate MAS running with ball work to mix physical and technical load.
Work from a marked central area in a 15-15 format: 15 seconds of MAS running, back and forth between the central area and the midfield, then 15 seconds of active recovery exchanging passes (strong foot, weak foot, on the move) before setting off again.
You train maximal aerobic speed while keeping your passing sharp under fatigue.
🎯 15-15 for 8 to 10 minutes.

⚽ An exercise idea to combine strength, footwork, possession and speed in an intermittent circuit.
Four or five groups of four rotate through the stations: jumps over medium and high hurdles; footwork in the hoops and between the poles; 4 vs 4 possession on a reduced pitch; a 20 m acceleration with a walk back. Look for quality on the jumps and footwork, no need to rush.
You train muscular power, coordination and aerobic capacity in blocks.
🎯 2-minute blocks with 1 minute rest between stations, for 12 minutes.

⚽ An exercise idea to keep your finishing sharp under fatigue, in intermittent format.
Four finishers and four servers rotate roles, starting from the centre. Position 1: head a ball thrown by hand. Positions 2 and 4: a ground pass, and the server calls the colour of the goal to hit. Position 3: finish a cross on the volley. Switch positions after each shot; most goals wins.
You train striking quality and composure when tired.
🎯 20-40 format (20 sec work / 40 sec rest) for 6 minutes.

⚽ An exercise idea to work offensive and defensive transition in 2 vs 2, with intermittent high-intensity efforts.
The keeper feeds a ball in behind to one of the two side players (red), who crosses for one of the other three to finish. The moment the action ends, the two central blues immediately break away on a counter to the far side against the two red side players. Four new players come in each sequence.
You train the instant switch between attacking and defending.
🎯 20-60 format (20 sec effort).

⚽ An exercise idea to build footwork solidity, core strength and the power to resist an opponent.
In pairs within a marked zone, one player protects a central cone in a low stance with an active core, the other stands behind. On the signal, the attacker tries to get round and touch the cone while the defender stays solid on their feet and blocks the path.
You train core stability, grounded footwork and duelling resistance.
🎯 Short, intense bouts of 15-30 sec for 7-8 minutes. Rotate roles each rep.

⚽ An exercise idea to build footwork solidity, core strength and the power to unbalance an opponent.
Pairs face each other in a small marked area, holding hands. On the signal, each player pushes, pulls and tries to knock the other off balance; the goal is to force your opponent out of the zone, sumo-style.
You train core strength, grip on the ground and raw duelling power.
🎯 Short, intense bouts of 15-30 sec for 7-8 minutes. Rotate roles and opponents.

⚽ An exercise idea to develop strength, commitment and aggression through 1 vs 1 with promotion and relegation.
Set up four pitches depending on numbers and play 1 vs 1 in one-minute sequences, with a minute's recovery. Run a promotion-relegation system: whoever scores most moves up a pitch, the other drops down.
You train physical commitment and the will to win every duel.
🎯 10-minute set.

⚽ An exercise idea to build lower-body strength through jumps and carry that power into your heading.
Work over small hurdles with repeated jumps, then finish each set immediately with a header.
You train jump power and transfer it straight into aerial finishing.
🎯 5-6 reps per player.

⚽ An exercise idea to build explosive strength and transfer it straight into maximum speed, in an attacker–defender duel.
Two players duel. Phase 1: both start seated on a bench and perform six vertical jumps for explosive strength. Phase 2: the attacker sprints with the ball toward goal while the defender sprints without it, through obstacles, hurdles and a slalom, to stop the shot.
You train explosive power and its immediate transfer to sprinting.
🎯 5-6 reps per player.

⚽ An exercise idea to develop perception, spatial awareness and command of different movement modes.
Players move in every direction across the area without touching any cones, varying how they travel: forward, backward, very fast, on tiptoe and on all fours.
You train perception, body organisation in space and varied motor control.

⚽ An exercise idea to link pass quality with footwork and coordination.
Two groups face each other. A player makes their pass, then performs a footwork drill (agility ladder, hoops or similar) before switching to the other side. The laps run continuously.
You connect clean passing with sharp coordination and motor skills.

⚽ An exercise idea to develop the five fundamental coordination qualities.
Move through five stations, one per quality: dissociation, with different work for the upper and lower body; balance, holding steady in varied situations; orientation, turning the body under different demands; reactivity, responding fast to signals; and rhythmicity, changing the tempo of your movements.
You train the full coordination toolkit that underpins every football action.

⚽ An exercise idea to build the ability to switch motor register fast while staying tuned into the game.
In a 3 vs 3 or 4 vs 4, the coach shows a colour every 30 seconds. For example: red, right foot only; yellow, left foot only; or red, one-touch, yellow, you must control. Players keep playing while watching the coach and switch register instantly.
You train quick adaptation and split attention, on the ball and on the cue.

⚽ An exercise idea to sharpen accuracy, precision and concentration in a darts-style game for football.
Mark out several zones like a dartboard, each worth different points, with the central zone worth the most. Players aim their strikes to land in the highest-scoring zones.
You train striking precision and focus under a scoring challenge.
🎯 The team with the most points wins.

⚽ A warm-up idea to work scanning over the shoulder and clean passing on the move.
Three teams; one player from each leaves the square and jogs around it. Inside, players pass to one another, but before receiving, the receiver must scan behind for the colour of the player running outside, and then pass to a teammate of that same colour. If no one is behind, they play freely.
You wake up your perception and passing while raising the tempo.
🎯 2 × 3 minutes, building intensity gradually.

⚽ A warm-up idea to read a teammate's run and pass cleanly in circulation.
In groups of eight, the first player passes to anyone and moves to a different cone. As the ball travels, the receiver watches where the passer runs and then plays into that space. For example, the green player passes to blue and takes the white column's spot, so blue must pass to the white player.
You sharpen your scanning and passing timing as the body warms up.
🎯 2 × 3 minutes, building intensity gradually.

⚽ A warm-up idea to wake up proprioception and stabilise your footwork through six progressive balance drills.
Thirty seconds each, two rounds (right leg then left). On one leg: eyes closed; touching cones with the free leg; then a partner feeds passes to control without losing balance. Next, single-leg forward jumps through the hoops, stabilising 3-4 seconds each; then the same laterally; then alternating forward.
You train balance, ankle stability and single-leg control.

⚽ A warm-up idea to raise body temperature by mixing running with varied technical exchanges in pairs.
In twos at a moderate pace, run while exchanging the ball: vary the type of pass (strong foot, weak foot, header) and the orientation (sideways, facing, forward and backward). Change exercise every minute.
You gradually warm the body while keeping the ball involved throughout.
🎯 7-8 minutes in total.

⚽ A warm-up idea to combine passing with footwork and coordination in a full, dynamic start.
The player passes the ball, performs a footwork-coordination drill, then takes the spot of the teammate they passed to, a different drill each time: agility ladder, hoops, markers, then poles.
You get the body moving while linking passes and coordination.
🎯 5-6 minutes.

⚽ A pre-season idea to switch the body back on with five ball circuits linked at a moderate pace.
Run the circuits continuously: left-foot slalom through the blue cones, right-foot slalom through the yellow, then both feet; a forward and backward circuit; and a full lap of the hoops, left foot round the blue, right round the yellow. Keep it easy, players should be able to talk while they run.
You rebuild aerobic base and ball feel gently after the break.
🎯 15 minutes at a moderate, conversational pace.

⚽ A pre-season idea to restart aerobic work in fartlek, alternating a moderate driving pace with faster exchanges.
Moderate pace, driving: right foot round the yellow-stake circuit, left foot round the green. Fast pace, exchanges: passes between two players facing each other, forward and backward. Keep switching between the two paces throughout.
You ease the aerobic system back up while keeping the ball in play.
🎯 15 minutes alternating both paces.

⚽ A pre-season idea to recover game intensity through three progressive possession-and-pressing games.
Game 1: 1 vs 3 in a small circle, 4 × 1 minute, the presser changing every minute. Game 2: 2 vs 4 in a marked square, 3 × 2 minutes, pressers changing every two minutes. Game 3: 4 vs 8 with four small goals on the sides, 3 × 3 minutes, pressers changing every three minutes.
You rebuild match sharpness in possession and pressing, step by step.

⚽ A pre-season idea to build possession and pressing through a sequence of evolving small-sided games, here for twelve players.
Game 1: 1 vs 3 in a small circle, 4 × 1 minute, the presser changing every minute. Game 2: 2 vs 4 in a marked square, 3 × 2 minutes, pressers changing every two minutes. Game 3: 4 vs 8 with four small goals on the sides, 3 × 3 minutes, pressers changing every three minutes.
You progressively load possession and pressing as the numbers grow.

⚽ A pre-season idea to combine strength, footwork, possession and speed in a simple circuit.
Four or five groups of four rotate through the stations: jumps over medium and high hurdles; footwork in the hoops and between poles; 4 vs 4 possession on a reduced pitch; a 20 m acceleration with a walk back. Look for quality on the jumps and footwork rather than speed.
You reload muscular power, coordination and aerobic capacity for the new season.
🎯 2-minute blocks with 1 minute rest between stations, for 12 minutes.

⚽ An exercise idea to finish after possession, and to turn a recovery into a shot when outnumbered.
Three teams of five in a 25×25 m zone with two goals on the sides, two teams together against one (10 vs 5). The overload of ten must string six consecutive passes, and whoever receives the sixth shoots at either goal. The five must win the ball back, and the recovering player shoots too; opponents can block.
You train finishing off possession and reactive strikes after regains.
🎯 3 × 4 minutes, changing the underload team each block. Most goals wins.

⚽ An exercise idea to link actions in front of goal, working the attacker–defender switch in changing numbers.
In a 25×20 m zone with two goals and keepers: Phase A, 2 vs 1, the attackers have 10 seconds to score and the defender can recover to attack. Phase B, 3 vs 2, two attackers enter and the former attackers become defenders. Phase C, back to 2 vs 1. The game runs non-stop, alternating 2 vs 1 and 3 vs 2.
You train fast projection to goal and instant role changes.
🎯 4-5 reps in both roles per player.

⚽ An exercise idea to develop reaction speed to a visual cue and finishing after a pressured sprint.
Two coloured cones, blue and red, sit on the sides with a ball at each. Players start their sprint, and mid-run the coach calls a colour, verbally or by showing a coloured cone, and can move around. Players read the cue, go to that side, recover the ball and score.
You train visual reaction, sprinting and composed finishing.

⚽ An exercise idea to sharpen reaction speed and finishing in front of goal.
Two players stand in a duel, face to face, doing quick small steps on the spot. At a random moment the coach rolls the ball between them; the instant they see it pass, they react, get to it first and shoot.
You train reactive quickness and the race to finish first.
🎯 4-5 reps per player. Minimum 45 seconds between reps.

⚽ An exercise idea to coordinate the runs of the wingers and central attackers.
Three groups, two wide and one central. On the wing, a player feeds a ball in behind to the corner, a second sprints on and crosses, then resets. In the middle, two players attack the box from the red cones while a third goes round the blue pole and sprints in to meet the cross. The wide and central runs must be timed so the move flows.
You train run timing and the finish on the cross.

⚽ An exercise idea to work 1 vs 1 intermittently across four pitches, with evolving rules and promotion.
In a 1 minute / 1 minute format, run a promotion-relegation mini-tournament where winners climb toward pitch 1. Each pitch has its own rule: pitch 4, keep the ball as long as possible; pitch 3, deposit it in the opponent's red zone; pitch 2, dribble across the opponent's goal line; pitch 1, score in one of the two small goals.
You train duelling quality and repeated high-intensity efforts.
🎯 8 games. Keep the intensity high.

⚽ An exercise idea to hold maximum intensity in 4 vs 4 on a small pitch with quick rotations.
Play 4 vs 4 on a small pitch with close goals, using three teams: two on, one waiting. Choose your rotation: a team comes off every two minutes, or the losing team comes off on each goal (or every two goals). Scatter balls around the pitch so play never stops when one goes out.
You train repeated sprints and duels at a relentless tempo.
🎯 Roughly 15-20 minutes total.

⚽ An exercise idea to develop rotating pressing and possession in 3 vs 1 between three teams.
Three teams of four on three pitches playing 3 vs 1. One player from each team goes to press another team, and the pressing player changes every 30 seconds. Keep the ball under pressure and hunt it back when it's your turn to press.
You train possession under pressure and coordinated ball-winning.
🎯 The team that recovers the most balls wins.

⚽ An exercise idea to work collective possession and accurate finishing under a six-pass constraint.
In a central zone, 5 vs 5 with four mini-goals on the sides. Once a team completes six consecutive passes, a player may finish first-time into any of the four mini-goals, but a team can't score twice in the same goal.
You train patient build-up and precise, one-touch finishing.
🎯 1 point per attempt, +3 if it goes in.

⚽ An exercise idea to work possession through a fun tic-tac-toe game.
Two teams keep the ball, with a tic-tac-toe grid beside the pitch. Every time a team strings eight passes, one player goes to place a ball, bib or cone on the grid, then rejoins, while their team plays a man short in the meantime. Keep everyone moving, no standing still.
You train keeping the ball and quick decisions while briefly outnumbered.
🎯 The team that forms a line on the grid wins.

⚽ An exercise idea to sharpen your defensive transition reflex, reaction speed and explosiveness.
In a 20×20 m zone with two small gates in the centre, an attacker takes on a defender and must dribble through a gate to score. The moment they cross a gate or lose the ball, an opposing attacker comes in and the old attacker becomes the defender, reacting as fast as possible to stop them.
You train the instant switch from attacking to defending.
🎯 1 point per gate crossed, 2 for winning the ball back in defence.

⚽ An exercise idea to work collective reactivity after losing the ball, pressing coordination and the defence-to-attack switch.
Three teams; two play 6 vs 6, one rests. The team in possession must complete eight passes before attacking the big goal with keeper. On loss, they press immediately, cutting passing lanes and closing the centre, while the team that recovers has just 5 seconds to attack.
You train immediate counter-pressing and rapid reorganisation.
🎯 2 points per successful press, 1 per goal in time. 3-minute sequences at maximum intensity.

⚽ An exercise idea to build the defensive transition reflex and collective reactivity to close the centre.
Two teams of six in a 30×40 m area with four mini-goals on the sides. One team keeps the ball; the other must win it and score in a mini-goal within 10 seconds. After a loss, the possession team presses immediately to stop them.
You train instant pressing and fast recovery once the ball is lost.
🎯 Possession: 1 point per 10+ passes, +1 if won back within 5 sec. Recovery: 1 per steal, +2 if scored within 10 sec. Phases of 2 minutes.

⚽ An exercise idea to develop offensive transition speed and defensive discipline after a loss, reproducing key match phases.
On two thirds of the pitch, the blues (8) attack the big goal in a positional attack and counter-press on loss, staying patient to find the right mismatch. The yellows (7) defend as a block, then counter fast toward the two mini-goals, aiming to finish within 10 seconds to exploit the disorganisation.
You train quick forward projection and rigorous rest-defence.
🎯 2 points per completed counter-attack, 1 for the positional attack.

⚽ An exercise idea to develop decision-making in transition, choosing between a fast break and a positional attack.
On half a pitch, the reds (7) attack the big goal in positional play; on loss they press, and if they win it high they go fast, otherwise they rebuild calmly. The greens (6) defend and counter on the three mini-goals. After each sequence the coach asks players to justify their choice.
You train reading the moment and picking the right transition.
🎯 Bonus point when the decision was the right one.

⚽ An exercise idea to work driving quality with both feet and the speed of ball circulation, in a competitive format.
Two concentric circuits. Circuit A (large circle): a both-footed slalom, left foot through the blue stakes, right through the yellow. Circuit B (inner circle): driving with your preferred foot, a full lap round the red stakes. On the signal players go for one minute, scoring a point for each completed lap.
You train close control in driving and quick, clean circulation.
🎯 1 point per full lap. Swap circles after a minute's recovery; most points wins.

⚽ An exercise idea to work long-pass quality (the switch) and chain speed through footwork and driving.
Two players on each side, across the pitch. The player on the right plays a switch to their partner, then goes to footwork on the ladder or hurdles. The other controls, drives to the poles, plays a ground pass back and returns backward to their spot. Do it as fast as you can.
You train raking switches and quick transitions between actions.
🎯 5-6 reps. Minimum 45 seconds between reps.

⚽ An exercise idea to combine collective passing quality with individual transition speed, competitively.
Two hexagons (or circles), six players each. The ball must travel a full lap and come back to the player who started it. As soon as it does and they control it, all the players sprint into the small central zone, the first team to get everyone in wins.
You train crisp passing under a race to finish first.
🎯 3-4 reps. 1 minute recovery between each.

⚽ An exercise idea to work few-touch passing quality and the pass-then-move chain, in a fun format.
Two teams of three to five, poles down the sides. Keep to a maximum of three passes per exchange, one or two touches. Each time you touch the ball you must quickly go round a pole on the side and hurry back onto the pitch.
You train sharp one- and two-touch passing linked to constant movement.
🎯 2 × 3 minutes.

⚽ An exercise idea to work oriented control and scanning behind before you receive.
Three players: one central without a ball, two on the sides with one. A side player passes in; the central player must open the ball out toward the red or yellow stake, deciding by scanning the player behind them (behind at red, play red; at yellow, play yellow). They return the ball, turn, and repeat with the other side.
You train the pre-scan and a clean, oriented first touch.

⚽ An exercise idea to work collective possession and the off-the-ball movement of wall players.
Two teams of eight in a 20×20 m zone: 4 vs 4 inside, four wall players each on the sides. To score, the possession team must string ten passes and use at least two wall players, who are attackable and must get a metre's lead on their marker to receive.
You train ball retention and creating angles off the ball.
🎯 1 point per ten passes with two wall players used. 10 minutes, rotating wall players every 2.

⚽ An exercise idea to work quick switches of play to exploit the goal the keeper leaves open.
A small-sided game (4 vs 4 up to 6 vs 6) with two small goals per team. One keeper per team defends both goals, set a category-appropriate distance apart, and tries to protect the one most at risk. That pushes you to accelerate and switch play fast to strike the unguarded mini-goal.
You train fast switches and reading where the space is.
🎯 2 × 6 minutes.

⚽ An exercise idea to work possession in numerical superiority and the transition to the other zone after a loss.
Two teams of seven on a half pitch split into two zones, 7 vs 5 in each. The possession team stays in its zone with the extra man; when the other team wins it, they move to the far zone, but two of the losing side must always stay put, so the team ends up outnumbered after each loss.
You train keeping the ball with an overload and reorganising on turnovers.
🎯 1 point every ten consecutive passes. 3 × 5 minutes.

⚽ An exercise idea to combine possession and progression through successive zones, building patience and precision.
In an 8 vs 4 across three successive zones, the overload team must clear all three stages to score, playing ten passes before advancing to the next zone, with two minutes to complete the three and score. Any loss sends them back to the starting zone.
You train patient positional play and progressing with control.
🎯 6 × 2 minutes, changing roles every 2.

⚽ An exercise idea to work collective transition between superiority and inferiority to sharpen game reading.
Small-sided games shifting from 4 vs 2 to 4 vs 6. Start 4 vs 2 in the centre, four blues, two pinks. As soon as the pinks win the ball it becomes 6 vs 4; when the blues recover, back to 4 vs 2, and so on.
You train adapting instantly as the numbers swing for and against you.
🎯 4 × 3 minutes, varying roles every 3.

⚽ An exercise idea to work possession and progression in a large space.
On a zone slightly bigger than half a pitch, one team defends its big goal, the other three small goals, with two wall players on the sides. To score, the possession team must complete ten consecutive passes and use both wall players. Stretching the effective space gives time and room, making possession and progression easier.
You train building and advancing while widening the pitch.
🎯 15 minutes, changing wall players every 2.

⚽ An exercise idea to develop progressing through successive zones in possession, using the wall players every time.
On a pitch split into two zones, eleven in possession (with two yellow wall players) face nine pressers. The possession team plays ten passes in zone 1 and uses both wall players, then moves into zone 2 to do the same, scoring three points if they succeed there. The pressers must win it and score fast.
You train zone-by-zone progression under real pressing.
🎯 3 points for succeeding in zone 2. 2 × 10 minutes, rotating wall players and roles.

⚽ An exercise idea to work collective speed of projection in numerical superiority to finish quickly.
Play 3 vs 2, the attackers starting from the halfway line and the defenders from the goal line. The attackers have a maximum of 10 seconds to score, so they must break forward fast and finish before the defence sets.
You train quick forward projection and decisive finishing on the break.
🎯 3-4 reps per player in both roles. Minimum 45 seconds between runs.

⚽ An exercise idea to work possession and the fast attack.
On a half pitch, two teams of six plus two joker support players on the sides. The red possession team may keep two unattackable players in the box to play out cleanly and must retain the ball as long as possible. The blue team only attacks: they must win it and score within 10 seconds.
You train playing out under pressure and striking quickly on the turnover.
🎯 2 × 10 minutes. Change wall players every 2, switch roles every 10. Most goals wins.

⚽ An exercise idea to carry possession and progression onto the full pitch, with no pressing inside the boxes.
Full pitch, two teams of eight plus two joker support players on the sides. The possession team must string ten passes and use both wall players before scoring, with one key restriction: no entering the opponent's boxes to press or score. Stretching the space gives time and room to build.
You train possession and progression over full-pitch distances.
🎯 2 × 10 minutes, changing wall players every 5.

⚽ An exercise idea for strikers to finish crosses on the volley or with the head, intermittently at high intensity.
In a zone twice the size of the box with two goals, two players work inside while two deliver crosses. The strikers volley or head the crosses in, switching sides after each shot.
You train aerial and volley finishing under a repeated high-intensity load.
🎯 45-45 or 30-30 format. 8-12 minutes.

⚽ An exercise idea for strikers to shoot quickly under pressure and finish a short duel in front of goal.
The player has 15 seconds to get a shot away; if the opponent wins the ball first, they can score. After 15 seconds the two swap roles.
You train fast, decisive finishing in a tight one-on-one.
🎯 15-45 (or 20-60) format. 7-8 minutes.

⚽ An exercise idea for strikers to time the run in behind and strike before the box, under a defender's pressure.
The attacker starts at the blue cone, a defender a metre behind at the red cone, a passer in the centre. The striker makes a checking run, then bursts in behind; as the run starts, the passer slips the ball through and the striker sprints on to shoot before the box, the defender chasing to block.
You train the timed run and a quick shot under pressure.
🎯 4-5 reps as attacker. Minimum 1 minute between reps.

⚽ An exercise idea for strikers to build a powerful start and carry that power into a shot on goal.
On the coach's signal the player runs about 10 metres while lightly held back by an elastic band or a partner to load the start, then is released to sprint onto the ball and finish.
You train an explosive first burst transferred straight into your strike.
🎯 4-5 reps per player. Minimum 45 seconds between reps.

⚽ An exercise idea for strikers to develop jump power and carry it into heading.
Work over the hurdles with repeated jumps, then finish each set with a header.
You train explosive jumping and transfer it into aerial finishing.
🎯 4-5 reps per player. Minimum 45 seconds between reps.

⚽ An exercise idea for midfielders to sharpen distribution, ground passes and switches, under fatigue and with scanning.
In a 25×25 m central square with four coloured zones, mini-goals and cones around it, a passer runs three chained actions: call a cone colour, and the central player touches it and recovers; a pass and a mini-goal call, controlled and played to that goal; then a pass and a zone call, controlled and switched to that zone.
You train quick scanning and accurate distribution while tired.

⚽ An exercise idea for the holding midfielder to recover fast against launched attackers and win the duel near the box.
In a 2 vs 2, the midfielders defend against two attackers running at them from close range. The defenders backpedal 4-5 metres, then brake to slow the attack; near the box the attackers can dribble, combine or shoot, and the midfielders must win the ball back.
You train backward speed, braking and duelling in front of the box.
🎯 4-5 reps as defender. Minimum 45 seconds between reps.

⚽ An exercise idea for midfielders to react to a mid-run signal and pass cleanly to a marked mini-goal.
Four coloured mini-goals sit on the sides. Players start sprinting, and mid-run the coach calls a colour, verbally or by showing a cone, and moving around, so players must track the coach, go to that side, recover the ball and pass into the matching mini-goal.
You train visual reaction on the move and precise distribution.
🎯 4-5 reps per player. Minimum 45 seconds between reps.

⚽ An exercise idea for midfielders to build a powerful start and carry it into an accurate switch.
On the signal, the player runs about 10 metres while lightly held back by an elastic band or a partner to load power, then is released, sprints onto the ball and plays a switch into one of the two zones called by the partner, blue or yellow.
You train explosive starts transferred into a raking switch.
🎯 4-5 reps per player. Minimum 45 seconds between reps.

⚽ An exercise idea for midfielders to work heading, ground passing and the forward-and-back chain, intermittently.
Two cones 20 m apart. Outbound: the midfielder reaches the red cone, the partner lofts a ball and calls a zone colour, and they head it toward that zone. Return: moving backward toward the green cone, the partner rolls a ground pass and calls a mini-goal colour, and with one touch they play it into that mini-goal.
You train aerial and ground distribution while shuttling under load.

⚽ An exercise idea for defenders to master the 1 vs 1 on the flank: jockeying, closing the angle and staying patient.
An attacker starts from the red cone on the flank, the defender from the penalty spot. Close the space first, then as the attacker nears, jockey at three-quarters and show them onto a tight angle where they can't shoot. Stay low with quick feet, use your arms, and wait patiently for the mistake to nick the ball.
You train defensive 1 vs 1 positioning and timing on the flank.

⚽ An exercise idea for defenders to win the flank 1 vs 1 by stopping the attacker crossing either gate.
In a 25×15 m flank zone with two red gates, one on the touchline and one near the box corner, the attacker starts at the red cone and the defender at the yellow. Close fast to deny them speed, channel and lock them toward the goal line, never facing them square-on, staying low with quick feet and using your arms.
You train jockeying and channelling in the wide 1 vs 1.

⚽ An exercise idea for defenders to react to a signal and time the intercepting tackle on the flank.
In a 15×15 m flank zone with two coloured mini-goals and two passers, the defender waits in the centre. The coach calls a colour; the defender rounds the matching cone, then intercepts that passer's ball aimed at the matching mini-goal, stepping across to tackle as you would on the flank, before recovering to the middle.
You train reading the pass and timing the intercepting tackle.
🎯 30-30 format. 6-8 minutes.

⚽ An exercise idea for defenders to move off the ball and give the keeper an option to start the build-up.
Four defenders work to your system of play; add attackers later for realism once the movements are sharp. The moment the keeper takes the ball at their feet, each defender must get free quickly to offer a passing option and set up the build-up you want.
You train scanning and getting free to open the first pass out.
🎯 4-5 reps. 45 seconds between reps.

⚽ An exercise idea for defenders to build power and reactivity and transfer that explosiveness into an accurate switch.
Three coloured cones sit three metres ahead. The coach calls a colour; the player reacts and touches that cone while a partner lightly holds them back to load power, then releases them. They run about 10 metres and play a switch into one of the three zones, blue, yellow or red, called by the coach.
You train reactive power transferred into a long, accurate switch.
🎯 4-5 reps per player. Minimum 45 seconds between reps.

⚽ An exercise idea for goalkeepers to react fast after an isometric hold and chain footwork with an exit.
Four coloured cones surround the keeper. Hold a squat for 10 seconds; the coach calls a colour to touch, then sends the ball as a lob or to the feet. The keeper reacts fast, touches the matching cone, then gets to the ball before its second bounce.
You train reaction speed out of a static hold and quick exits.
🎯 5-6 reps. Minimum 45 seconds between reps.

⚽ An exercise idea for goalkeepers to switch from a static hold to an explosive, reactive dive.
Hold an isometric squat for 10 seconds with your back to play, then spin into an explosive, reactive dive. To keep it unpredictable, the coach can clip equipment on the pitch as they shoot to deflect the ball's path.
You train the jump from stillness to an explosive save.
🎯 5-6 reps. Minimum 45 seconds between reps.

⚽ An exercise idea for goalkeepers to react to a visual cue and speed up the first handling action.
Stand 1.5-2 metres from the coach, who holds a ball in each hand. They drop one without warning, and the keeper must touch it before it hits the ground.
You train pure reaction speed and fast hands to the ball.
🎯 5-6 reps. Minimum 45 seconds between reps.

⚽ An exercise idea for goalkeepers to develop vertical reach and precise, fast distribution to a called target.
Start seated on a bench with a high hurdle in front and two coloured mini-goals on the sides. Explode up and over the hurdle, catch the ball at its highest point, and as you take it the coach calls a mini-goal colour; the moment you land, send the ball fast to that goal.
You train high catching and accurate distribution under time pressure.
🎯 5-6 reps. Minimum 45 seconds between reps.

⚽ An exercise idea for goalkeepers to build footwork power and transfer it into a reactive lateral dive.
Perform three crouched jumps with the legs bent, then dive right or left. The ball is sent to one side at the last moment, and the keeper must touch it before it crosses the blue line.
You train leg power and its transfer into an explosive dive.
🎯 5-6 reps. Minimum 45 seconds between reps.

⚽ An exercise idea to work speed with and without the ball, mixing driving, running and quick thinking in a tic-tac-toe game.
Two teams of four or five in a relay, with a nine-square grid. Each player works speed with the ball in a slalom or straight line, fetches a cone or bib in their team colour, places it in an empty square, then comes back and passes to the next player, who sets off on receiving.
You train ball-driving speed linked to fast decisions.
🎯 The first team to form a line wins.

⚽ An exercise idea to work speed with changes of direction and the 2 vs 2 duel against the clock.
In a 2 vs 2, attackers start from the halfway line and defenders from the goal line. After a short opening slalom, the attackers have a maximum of 6 seconds to score.
You train sharp changes of direction and quick finishing under time pressure.
🎯 3-4 reps in both roles. Minimum 45 seconds between reps.

⚽ An exercise idea to develop futsal-specific intermittent endurance, alternating footwork and short shuttle runs.
Alternate footwork in a 5-25 format with short shuttle runs in 10-20, adjusting the distances to each player.
You train repeated efforts and quick feet in a futsal-specific rhythm.
🎯 Total 10 minutes.

⚽ An exercise idea to combine footwork and strength work with finishing.
Players run back and forth, working footwork or strength in the middle of the route, then shoot on each side, alternating a strike on the big goal and one into a lateral mini-goal.
You train quick feet, strength and finishing in one continuous effort.
🎯 30-30 format (30 sec work / 30 sec rest). 8 minutes.

⚽ An exercise idea to work rational use of space in futsal, capping each team at two players per zone.
Play 4 vs 4 with keepers on a full pitch split into four zones. The key rule: never more than two players from the same team in the same zone, which forces you to spread out and occupy the space sensibly. Switch positions every two minutes.
You train balanced spacing and disciplined positional occupation.
🎯 Total 10 minutes.
